desc: Kraków combines a remarkably preserved medieval core with major museums, neighborhood food scenes, and easy regional day trips, making it one of Central Europe's most complete short breaks. Visitor pressure peaks in late spring, summer weekends, and festive December periods, while winter (outside holidays) is quieter and often better value.
hls: UNESCO old town, Rynek Główny, and Wawel complex
Deep history from medieval Poland to modern-era memory sites
Strong museum range plus café and cellar-bar culture
Walkable districts including Kazimierz and Podgórze
Easy links to Wieliczka, Auschwitz, and Zakopane
Popular Christmas-market and festive-season city break

todo.name: Old Town & Wawel loop
todo.desc: Walk from Rynek Główny to Wawel Castle and cathedral to anchor your first day in Kraków.

todo.name: Kazimierz neighborhoods
todo.desc: Explore synagogues, street art, and independent cafés in Kraków's historic Jewish quarter.

todo.name: Cloth Hall + museum picks
todo.desc: Mix main-square landmarks with one focused museum visit (e.g., Czartoryski or MOCAK).

todo.name: Vistula riverside evening
todo.desc: Use the boulevards and bars for an easy sunset walk in warmer months.

todo.name: Wieliczka Salt Mine
todo.desc: Half-day underground chambers and carvings; reserve ahead in peak season.

todo.name: Seasonal market crawl
todo.desc: Target Easter fairs, summer courtyards, or the strong Advent/Christmas market period.

wifi.note: Excellent in central hotels, apartments, and coworking hubs; one of the easiest regional cities for remote-work reliability.

prac.fltNote: Gatwick–Kraków is well served year-round with frequent low-cost competition. Return fares are often ~£30–£85 off-peak, ~£85–£150 in shoulder demand, and ~£150+ during Easter, summer weekends, and December market/holiday peaks.

airports.name: Kraków John Paul II International Airport

prac.visa: Poland is in Schengen; UK and US passport holders can typically visit visa-free for up to 90 days in any 180-day period.
alerts: Old Town accommodation and restaurant pricing rises quickly on festival and holiday weekends.
Popular day tours (Wieliczka/Auschwitz) can sell out in peak months; reserve slots in advance.
Cold snaps and occasional snow can slow morning transport in winter despite good core walkability.

sweet: May–June and September are the best all-round months; early December is strong for festive atmosphere if you prebook central stays.
